title: Elrendar Fellowship Guild Hall
summary_html: The holdings of the Elrendar Fellowship, nestled along the banks of the river of the same name.

title: Stormwind City
summary_html: Capital of the Alliance, and humanity on Azeroth.

title: Gnomeregan
summary_html: Capital of the gnomes on Azeroth.

title: Thunder Totem
summary_html: Capital of the Highmountain Tauren, located deep in the mountains of the Broken Isles.

title: Thunder Totem
summary_html: Capital of the Highmountain Tauren, located deep in the mountains of the Broken Isles.

title: The Exodar
summary_html: Capital of the Draenei exiles on Azeroth.

title: Orgrimmar
summary_html: Capital of the Horde, and the orcs on Azeroth.

title: Thunderbluff
summary_html: The capital of the tauren of the Horde.

title: Ahn'Qiraj
summary_html: A stronghold of the qiraji, servants of the Old Gods and the Black Empire.

title: Gadgetzan
summary_html: A goblin town under the rule of the Steamwheedle Cartel.

title: Bloodhoof Village
summary_html: A small township of the tauren in Mulgore.

title: Mereldar
summary_html: The main foothold of the Arathi in Hallowfall.

title: Wyrmrest Tower
summary_html: An ancient titan structure, home to the Wyrmrest Accord during its governance throughout the war against the Scourge.

title: Wintergrasp Fortress
summary_html: An ancient titan stronghold which has since become a hotbed of conflict between the Horde and Alliance.

title: Argent Tournament Grounds
summary_html: The neutral tournament grounds of the Argent Crusade, a place where both the Horde, Alliance, and Ebon Blade joined forces with the Crusade for the final march on Icecrown.

title: Darnassus
summary_html: Once the capital of the Kaldorei on Kalimdor, it was sacked and burned to a husk by the Horde.

title: Ironforge City
summary_html: The capital of the Bronzebeard dwarves of the Alliance.

title: Silvermoon City
summary_html: The eternal capital of the elves of Quel'Thalas. Once destroyed, it has since been rebuilt to its former glory.

title: Thalassian University
summary_html: The grand university of the Thalassian Magisterium, based in Silvermoon City. The school is one of the premier magical institutions on Azeroth, and often considered the only proper rival of the Kirin Tor's Dalaran before its destruction.

title: Sunwell Plateau
summary_html: The sanctuary holdings of the Sin'dorei, where historic Sunwell rests on the Isle of Quel'Danas.

title: Undercity
summary_html: The recently reclaimed capital of the Forsaken, and once capital of humanity in Lordaeron. Blight still taints its halls, but clean up is ongoing.

title: Light's Hope Chapel
summary_html: One of the chief holdings of the Argent Crusade, and the Order of the Silver Hand.

title: Menethil Harbor
summary_html: One of the Alliance's main ports in the middle half of the continent.

title: Gilneas City
summary_html: The recently reclaimed capital of the worgen, and the historic home of the Gilnean people.

title: Stromgarde City
summary_html: One of the Alliance's reclaimed holdings in the northern half of the continent; it is ruled by Danath Trollbane.

title: Hammerfall
summary_html: A Horde fortification that once served as an internment camp of the orcs after the Second War.

title: Grim Batol
summary_html: Once the mighty kingdom of the Wildhammer Dwarves, the Second War saw its vast halls cursed with shadows, and it has since fallen into the hands of many evils.

title: Hearthglen
summary_html: One of the chief holdings of the Argent Crusade, and the Order of the Silver Hand.

title: Zul'Aman
summary_html: The seat of the Amani Empire, the perennial enemy of the Quel'dorei and Sin'dorei elves.

title: Zul'Gurub
summary_html: The seat of Gurubashi Empire, or at least what remains of it.

title: Thas'loren
summary_html: An elven destroyer made accessible to the Fellowship to continue its work.

title: Elrendar Fellowship Guild Hall
summary_html: The holdings of the Elrendar Fellowship, nestled along the banks of the river of the same name.

title: Autumnveil Forge
summary_html: The forge and smithy of Haldarin Autumnveil, providing services to the Fellowship.

title: Training Grounds
summary_html: A training grounds, armory, and jail for dangerous persons captured by the Fellowship and held for a short time. Maintained by the Blood Knights, the Fellowship works hand in hand to ensure its effectively utilized.

title: Medical Clinic
summary_html: The medical clinic, maintained by Priestess Kynseria Vel'dinariel and the other healers of the Fellowship and used for all manner of treatments.

title: Relic Vault
summary_html: A relic vault maintained by Blood Knight Tal'theas Sunfeather, where the Fellowship has begun to store many power and dangerous relics and artifacts.

title: Jendral's Gem Shop
summary_html: A gem smithing and jeweler's shop, maintained by the artisan Jendral Rosepath.

title: Lightstrike Apothecary
summary_html: An apothecary's shop maintained by Allithndria Lightstriker, a fellow of the order.

title: Greenhouse & Tinctures
summary_html: A greenhouse and herbalist shop maintained by Lark and Finch.

title: Event Hall
summary_html: A large, multifunctional event hall.

title: Hearth's Home Tavern
summary_html: A large tavern nestled in the woods near the guild hall.

title: Breaktusk Hearth
summary_html: A large tavern nestled in the woods near the guild hall, maintained by Bel'thar Breaktusk.

title: The Dark Portal
summary_html: The Dark Portal was built to open the way for the orcs to invade Azeroth, and it has since been opened a closed a half dozen times, heralding doom and chaos with each new awakening.

title: The Stair of Destiny
summary_html: The great gateway built by enslaved Draenei, the orcs intended to invade new worlds through it, Azeroth first before all others.

title: Blackrock Foundry
summary_html: A massive foundry and fortress that serves as the seat of power for the Blackrock Orc clan on Draenor.

title: Highmaul Fortress
summary_html: One of the last remaining ogre strongholds on Draenor, Highmaul is the seat of the Imperator's power and from where the ogres wage war against any who oppose their power.

title: Oshu'gun
summary_html: The crashed interdimensional ship known as the Genedar, it collided with the ground some two hundred years before the opening of the Dark Portal when the Draenei arrived on Draenor. It is revered by the orcs as a holy mountain within much many of their ancestors' spirits now reside.

title: Ashran
summary_html: An island east of the Dark Portal heavily fought over by the Horde and Alliance and ogres alike, this would have been the home of Deathwing the Destroyer had he arrived in this timeline, but in his absence it has simply become a hotly contested battleground.

title: Shadowmoon Crypts
summary_html: The burial grounds of the Shadowmoon orc clan on Draenor, saturated with dark void magic.

title: The Stair of Destiny
summary_html: The Outland facing side of the Dark Portal, a massive gateway linking Azeroth to both the Outlands, and Draenor.

title: Calcydo's Home
summary_html: A home belonging to fellow Calcydo.

title: Syrin's Home
summary_html: A home belonging to fellow Syrin.

title: Silanna's Home
summary_html: A home belonging to fellow Silanna Leybinder.

title: Tulindi's Home
summary_html: A home belonging to fellow Tulindi de Coeur-Errant.

title: Venymir's Home
summary_html: A home belonging to fellow Venymir Stormwalker.

title: Alen'dris's Home
summary_html: A home belonging to fellow Alen'dris Sunblossom.

title: Laelisel's Home
summary_html: A home belonging to fellow Laelisel Goldsong.

title: Glor'andal's Home
summary_html: A home belonging to fellow Glor'andal Druindar.

title: Jeshacinin's Home
summary_html: A home belonging to fellow Jeshacinin Evertrail.

title: Ashadarie's Home
summary_html: A home belonging to fellow Ashadarie Dawncrest.

title: Onitheril's Home
summary_html: A home belonging to fellow Onitheril.

title: Portal Gate
summary_html: A portal gate located near one of Quel'Thalas' powerful runestones.

title: Scholomance
summary_html: A long standing stronghold of the undead, these halls have served as a regional seat of power for the Scourge.

title: Suramar City
summary_html: Capital city of the Shal'dorei, highborne elves who survived the Sundering by sealing their city away under a magical dome.

title: Suramar City
summary_html: Capital city of the Shal'dorei, highborne elves who survived the Sundering by sealing their city away under a magical dome.

title: Dalaran
summary_html: During the Burning Legion's third invasion, the Kirin Tor teleported the city of Dalaran over the Broken Isles.

title: Dalaran
summary_html: During the Burning Legion's third invasion, the Kirin Tor teleported the city of Dalaran over the Broken Isles.

title: Dalaran
summary_html: During the Nexus War and the campaigns against the Scourge, the Kirin Tor teleported the city of Dalaran over Northrend.

title: City of Dornogal
summary_html: Capital of the isle of Dorn, where the Earthen govern the security of the Coreway leading to Azeroth's heart.

title: Oribos
summary_html: The central conduit for all souls entering the Shadowlands, Oribos has become an interdimensional waygate in recent times.

title: Warsong Hold
summary_html: The Horde's's main foothold in Northrend during the war against the Lich King.

title: Valiance Keep
summary_html: The Alliance's main foothold in Northrend during the war against the Lich King.

title: Vengeance Landing
summary_html: The Horde's's secondary foothold in Northrend during the war against the Lich King.

title: Valgarde Keep
summary_html: The Alliance's secondary foothold in Northrend during the war against the Lich King.

title: Utgarde Keep
summary_html: One of several main holdings of the vrykul in Northrend, taken over by the San'layn during the Lich King's rise to power.

title: Ulduar
summary_html: The mighty Titan city of Ulduar, it is one of the Titan Keeper's greatest bastions of power on Azeroth, and was also the prison of Yogg'saron, one of the Old Gods who once ruled Azeroth.

title: Amirdrassil
summary_html: The newest world tree, planted and grown by the dragons on their home isle; it is intended as a replacement for Teldrassil, which was burned by the Horde, but few Kaldorei truly believe it is their new home.

title: Vordrassil
summary_html: Once one of the mighty World Trees, it was felled after falling to the corruption of the Old Gods.

title: Molten Cay
summary_html: An island off the coast of Kalimdor, in the South Seas.

title: Verdant Wilds
summary_html: An island off the coast of Kalimdor, in the South Seas.

title: The Lost Isle
summary_html: An uncharted island in the South Seas, near Kezan.

title: Kezan
summary_html: An isle in the South Seas, once home to the Bilgewater Cartel goblins before the eruption of the island's volcano.

title: The Maelstrom
summary_html: A violent, churning maw which stands as a permanent reminder of the terrible Sundering which shattered Azeroth's main landmass.

title: Mechagon
summary_html: An island off the coast of the Kul Tiran mainland, it is home to the mechagnomes who have since joined forces with the Alliance.

title: Kul Tiras
summary_html: The Kul Tiran mainland, home to the human nation of the same name; it has long been the preeminent naval power on Azeroth.

title: Boralus
summary_html: The capital city of the Kul Tiran kingdom, long since under the rule of the Proudmoore family.

title: Boralus
summary_html: The capital city of the Kul Tiran kingdom, long since under the rule of the Proudmoore family.

title: The Broken Isles
summary_html: A chain of islands once central to the ancient Kaldorei Empire, since the Sundering it has since come to the attention of the Burning Legion.

title: Jorundall
summary_html: An island off the coast of Kalimdor, in the South Seas.

title: Havenwood
summary_html: An island off the coast of Kalimdor, in the South Seas.

title: Crestfall
summary_html: An island off the coast of the Kul Tiran mainland, it has been a battleground between the Horde and Alliance for decades.

title: Tol Barad
summary_html: An island fortress of the human kingdoms in the east, it has been a battleground between the Horde and Alliance for decades.

title: Crestfall
summary_html: An island off the coast of the Kul Tiran mainland, it has been a battleground between the Horde and Alliance for decades.

title: Mechagon
summary_html: An island off the coast of the Kul Tiran mainland, it is home to the mechagnomes who have since joined forces with the Alliance.

title: Kul Tiras
summary_html: The Kul Tiran mainland, home to the human nation of the same name; it has long been the preeminent naval power on Azeroth.

title: Shattrath City
summary_html: Once a gleaming capital of the Draenei's civilization on Draenor, the city was sacked and burned by the Horde, and has since been claimed by refugees and wanderers, and of all beings-the naaru.

title: Honor Hold
summary_html: An Alliance stronghold built during the Alliance Expedition's forray through the Dark Portal. It survived twenty years after the destruction of the planet, providing much needed shelter to the survivors.

title: Thrallmar
summary_html: A Horde stronghold built by the Horde during its charge through the Dark Portal to the Outlands, during its conflict against the Burning Legion.

title: Hellfire Citadel
summary_html: A crumbling stronghold of the Horde before its invasion of Azeroth, it has had many masters, but none of them who intended to do good with the power it afforded.

title: Hellfire Citadel
summary_html: A crumbling stronghold of the Horde before its invasion of Azeroth, it has had many masters, but none of them who intended to do good with the power it afforded.

title: Shattrath City
summary_html: The gleaming capital of the Draenei's civilization on Draenor, the Iron Horde attempted to destroy the city, but failed.

title: Auchindoun
summary_html: The grand mausoleum of the Draenei, the soul priests tend to the honored dead and preserve their memory.

title: Auchindoun
summary_html: Once the grand mausoleum of the Draenei, the soul priests who tended to the honored dead here have become twisted, and haunt the ruins. The structure was destroyed by the Shadow Council during the Alliance's initial surge through the Dark Portal during the Alliance Expedition.

title: Temple of Karabor
summary_html: One of the holiest places on Draenor for the Draenei who built it, the temple at Karabor is the mighty spirit of their civilization, and from where the Prophet Velen led his people.

title: Bladespire Citadel
summary_html: Once a seat of power for the ogres of Draenor, the Horde aided the Frostwolf clan in claiming the mighty fortress.

title: Karazhan, Tower of the Guardian
summary_html: Built by the Guardian Aegwynn, it has since stood as a seat for the Guardian of Azeroth, though it brought ruin upon the world under the reign of Medivh.

title: Dragonmaw Port
summary_html: A seat of power for the Horde in the Twilight Highlands, established by Warchief Garrosh Hellscream and Dragonmaw Warlord Zaela.

title: Booty Bay
summary_html: A trading port in the southern Eastern Kingdoms ruled by the Steamwheedle goblin cartel.

title: Bloodsail Bucaneers Fleet
summary_html: A known anchorage for the many ships of the Bloodsail Bucaneers, a fierce gang of pirate marauders roaming the seas of Azeroth.

title: Highbank
summary_html: A seat of power for the Alliance in the Twilight Highlands, established during the ongoing conflict with the Horde.

title: Strand of the Ancients
summary_html: The site of ancient Titan ruins over which the Horde and Alliance have long fought for the high ground.

title: Isle of Conquest
summary_html: An island off the coast of Northrend where the Horde and Alliance have long battled for dominance over resources.

title: Northrend
summary_html: The frozen crown of the world, Northrend is a land of danger and despair, with the influence of the Old Gods, the Scourge, and many other powers at play.

title: Isle of Dorn
summary_html: The Isle of Dorn is the crown of Khaz Algar, a region of Azeroth home to the Earthen, keepers of the Titans' creations. The capital city of Dornogal sits in the shadow of the nearby mountain.

title: Forbidden Reach
summary_html: An island off the coast of the Dragon Isles, where the vaults of the Dracthyr, creations of Neltharion, reside.

title: Dragon Isles
summary_html: An island long shrouded from the world by dense mist, it's awakened since the wounding of the world during the final battle against the Burning Legion.

title: Tol Barad
summary_html: An island fortress of the human kingdoms in the east, it has been a battleground between the Horde and Alliance for decades.

title: Kargath
summary_html: A Horde outpost located in the barren wastes known as the Badlands.

title: Thelsamar
summary_html: A dwarven outpost located on the shores of Loch Modan.

title: Blackrock Mountain
summary_html: Once a Dark Iron mountain fortress, the Second War saw the invasion of the orcish Horde, and the upper spires were captured and used as a final holdout till long after the war's end. In Shadowforge City far below ground, the Dark Irons still hold sway.

title: Shadowforge City
summary_html: The capital city of the Dark Iron dwarves, buried far below lava scorced landscape of Searing Gorge.

title: Lakeshire
summary_html: An Alliance township on the lake shores of the Redridge Mountains.

title: Darkshire
summary_html: An Alliance township nestled in the depths of Duskwood, once known as Brightwood.

title: Sentinel Hill
summary_html: An Alliance township settled on the central heights of Westfall.

title: Grom'gol
summary_html: A Horde settlement on the western coasts of Stranglethorn Vale.

title: Marshtide Watch
summary_html: An Alliance settlement deep within the swampy Black Morass, near the Sunken Temple of the Gurubashi.

title: Stonard
summary_html: A Horde settlement deep within the swampy Black Morass, near the Sunken Temple of the Gurubashi

title: Nethergarde Keep
summary_html: An Alliance fortress initially constructed to keep watch over the Dark Portal.

title: Orkil'on Hold
summary_html: A Horde fortress built to counter the Alliance's influence within the Blasted Lands near the Dark Portal.

title: Tarren Mill
summary_html: A Horde outpost built upon the ruins of an Alliance township destroyed during the Third War.

title: Brill
summary_html: A Horde township razed during the Alliance's assault on the Undercity following the Horde's destruction of Teldrassil.

title: Goldenmist Village
summary_html: An elven township on the shores of the Elrendar River.

title: Tranquillien
summary_html: An elven township south of the Elrendar River.

title: Suncrown Village
summary_html: An elven township on the shores of the Elrendar River.

title: Fairbreeze Village
summary_html: An elven village east of Sunsail Anchorage.

title: Falthrien Academy
summary_html: A prestigeous academy of the arcane arts within Quel'Thalas.

title: Sunsail Anchorage
summary_html: A prominent anchorage at the mouth of the northern branch of the Elrendar River in Quel'Thalas.

title: Dawnstar Village
summary_html: A prominent settlement just north of the Sunwell Plateau.

title: Windrunner Village
summary_html: A prominent settlement along the western coast of southern Quel'Thalas.

title: Magisters Terrace
summary_html: A prestigeous enclave and repository of arcane knowledge, and the prime dominion of Quel'Thalas' magistry.

title: Thorium Point
summary_html: A neutral outpost governed by the Thorium Brotherhood of the Dark Iron dwarves.

title: Greymane Wall Gate
summary_html: The shattered gateway leading into the human kingdom of Gilneas, home to the noble worgen.

title: Refuge Point
summary_html: An Alliance outpost guarding the entrance to the Arathi Basin

title: Havenwood
summary_html: An island off the coast of Kalimdor, in the South Seas.

title: Astranaar
summary_html: A Kaldorei settlement nestled deep in the forests of Ashenvale.

title: New Feathermoon Stronghold
summary_html: A Kaldorei stronghold on the western coast of Kalimdor.

title: Eldre'thalas
summary_html: An ancient, ruined Kaldorei stronghold once home to the Shendralar.

title: Crossroads
summary_html: A Horde outpost in the central barrens of Kalimdor.

title: Ruins of Theramore
summary_html: The ruins of Theramore City, which was bombed into non-existence by the Horde.

title: Eastern Kingdoms
summary_html: The eastern most continental landmass of Azeroth, home to dwarves, elves, gnomes, humans, and the undead, among other creatures.

title: Teldrassil
summary_html: A world tree initially planted without the blessing of the dragon aspects, Teldrassil became a home for the Kaldorei people in the wake of the Third War, until it was destroyed by the Horde.

title: Azuremyst & Bloodmyst Isles
summary_html: The eastern most continental landmass of Azeroth, home to dwarves, elves, gnomes, humans, and the undead, among other creatures.

title: Molten Cay
summary_html: An island off the coast of Kalimdor, in the South Seas.

title: Verdant Wilds
summary_html: An island off the coast of Kalimdor, in the South Seas.

title: Snowblossom Village
summary_html: An island off the northern coast of Pandaria, home to the eponymous Snowblossom Village.

title: Timeless Isle
summary_html: An island off the eastern coast of Pandaria, where the Ancient Celestials held court during the conflict between the Horde and Alliance.

title: Rotting Mire
summary_html: An island off the southeastern coast of Pandaria, where the Horde and Alliance have battled for control over resources.

title: Pandaria
summary_html: The Pandaren mainland, which was shrouded in mist for some ten thousand years since the Sundering of Azeroth.

title: Isle of Thunder
summary_html: An island off the northern coast of Pandaria where Le'shen, the Thunder King, ruled for an age.

title: Isle of Giants
summary_html: An island off the northern coast of Pandaria where the skeletons of giant beasts paint pictures of life before the Sundering of Azeroth.

title: Zandalar
summary_html: The island home and last remaining bastion of the Zandalari Empire, a nation of trolls that ruled long before the Sundering of Azeroth.

title: Dazar'alor
summary_html: Dazar'alor, the capital of the Zandalari Empire, has long stood as a shining example of the olden days when trolls ruled Azeroth.

title: Icecrown Citadel
summary_html: The Scourge's true seat of power, a massive fortress built up around Frozen Throne's glacier. From here, the Lich King sought to dominant the world.

title: Neltharion's Lair
summary_html: Once a vast underground cavern that served as home to Neltharion, once the Earth Warder turned Destroyer.

title: Neltharion's Lair
summary_html: Once a vast underground cavern that served as home to Neltharion, once the Earth Warder turned Destroyer.

title: Blackrook Hold Crypts
summary_html: The entrance to the crypts of the Blackrook Hold, where forces of the Horde and Alliance stormed the tower to throw back the occupying Legion forces.

title: Blackrook Hold Crypts
summary_html: The entrance to the crypts of the Blackrook Hold, where forces of the Horde and Alliance stormed the tower to throw back the occupying Legion forces.

title: Sea Gate to Helheim
summary_html: A sea gate through which the Naglfar, the ship of the dead helmed by the kvaldir, ferries captured souls of slain vrykul to Helheim, the dominion of Helya.

title: Sea Gate to Helheim
summary_html: A sea gate through which the Naglfar, the ship of the dead helmed by the kvaldir, ferries captured souls of slain vrykul to Helheim, the dominion of Helya.

title: Halls of Valor
summary_html: A massive gateway leading through

title: Helheim
summary_html: The main gateway leading down into the realm of Helheim, the dominion of the fallen Keeper Helya, now a sorceress in service to death itself.

title: Halls of Valor
summary_html: A massive gateway leading through

title: Helheim
summary_html: The main gateway leading down into the realm of Helheim, the dominion of the fallen Keeper Helya, now a sorceress in service to death itself.

title: Valdrakken
summary_html: The recently reclaimed seat of the Dragon Aspects, and capital for all dragons on Azeroth.

title: Roots of Teldrassil
summary_html: The charred roots of Teldrassil, one of the world trees, which stretch deep into the realm of Harandar.

title: Roots of Shaladrassil
summary_html: The recovering roots of Shaladrassil, one of the world trees, which stretch deep into the realm of Harandar.

title: Roots of Nordrassil
summary_html: The thriving roots of Nordrassil, one of the world trees, which stretch deep into the realm of Harandar.

title: Roots of Amirdrassil
summary_html: The blossoming roots of Amirdrassil, one of the world trees, which stretch deep into the realm of Harandar.

title: The Rift of Aln
summary_html: The rift of Aln, a sacred and mystical realm that has been affected by the Nightmare in recent years.

title: Slayer's Rise
summary_html: A blood stained battleground fought over by both the Horde and Alliance, and also the violent and wild forces of the void.

title: The Voidspire
summary_html: The seat of Xal'atath's power in the Voidstorm.
